skill.name: managing-docs
skill.description: Organizes documentation structures, documentation site configuration, and project documentation standards for software teams.
skill.summary: Plan, restructure, and configure project documentation systems.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and blocker patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown fences, documentation file names, and example configuration text. The real residual risk is that the skill requests Bash plus write/edit access and recommends package install, build, and deploy commands. No evidence of prompt injection, credential theft, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Broad Bash Access With Install and Deploy Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash access and includes commands for installing documentation tools, building sites, and deploying with mkdocs. These commands are legitimate for documentation management, but they can change the environment or publish content if run without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool list and command examples are explicit. The context is normal documentation tooling, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Modification Is Central to the Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to create, move, edit, and consolidate documentation files. This is expected behavior, but it can affect many project files and should be used with normal review of diffs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Write and Edit are declared tools, and the instructions explicitly describe moving and removing duplicate documentation. The capability is legitimate but has real file-change impact.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Benign Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The detected URLs are a placeholder domain in a Docusaurus configuration example and a public markdownlint repository in a pre-commit example. No evidence shows data exfiltration or calls to an unknown service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs appear inside configuration examples for documentation tooling. I found no surrounding instructions to transmit secrets or contact the URLs at runtime.

low_findings.title: Sensitive and Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static findings for weak cryptography and the Windows SAM database are caused by documentation terms such as Markdown filenames, API reference text, and SECURITY.md. No evidence of cryptographic implementation, password database access, or credential collection was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are visible documentation examples and file names, not executable code. There is no semantic evidence of SAM access or weak cryptographic use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands in Markdown Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for package installation and documentation deployment. Users should approve these commands intentionally because they can install packages, run local services, or publish documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and could have side effects if executed. They are presented as normal examples, so this is a usage warning rather than a malicious pattern.

content.value_statement: Unstructured documentation makes projects harder to maintain and onboard. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan clear documentation structures and tool configurations.
actual_capabilities: Recommends documentation layouts for small projects, large projects, and monorepos.
Maps documentation types such as reference, concepts, how-to guides, and tutorials.
Provides setup guidance for MkDocs, Docusaurus, Sphinx, and TypeDoc.
Defines file naming conventions and required project documentation files.
Supports migration and consolidation plans for existing documentation.
Suggests automation patterns for documentation deployment and linting.
limitations: It does not verify whether generated documentation content is technically accurate.
It may propose shell commands that require user review before execution.
It does not replace a full documentation audit for large regulated projects.
It focuses on structure and tooling rather than long-form content writing.

use_cases.title: Create a documentation structure
use_cases.description: Plan a docs directory, navigation model, and required files for a new software project.

use_cases.title: Standardize documentation across packages
use_cases.description: Define shared conventions for README files, package docs, references, and contribution guides.

use_cases.title: Prepare a documentation site
use_cases.description: Choose a documentation generator and draft configuration patterns for a publishable site.

prompt_templates.title: Suggest a basic docs layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project and suggest a simple documentation structure with required files and short reasons for each file.

prompt_templates.title: Organize existing documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the current documentation folders and propose a cleaner structure. Include files to move, merge, or rename.

prompt_templates.title: Configure a documentation site
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend a documentation site generator for this project and outline the configuration, navigation, and build workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Design a monorepo documentation system
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a documentation architecture for this monorepo. Cover package docs, shared guides, versioning, automation, and ownership rules.

output_examples.input: Plan documentation for a small library.
output: A README-centered structure with quick start, installation, usage, and contribution files.
A small docs folder for API details and troubleshooting.
Naming rules that keep files lowercase and easy to link.
A migration plan that separates tutorials, guides, concepts, and reference pages.
A list of duplicate pages to consolidate into canonical versions.
Navigation recommendations for a documentation site.
A suggested build and deployment workflow for documentation changes.
Pre-commit linting guidance for Markdown files.
A warning to review deployment commands before running them.

best_practices: Review proposed file moves and command execution before applying changes.
Choose one canonical location for each documentation topic.
Keep navigation aligned with user tasks, not internal team structure.
anti_patterns: Do not scatter setup instructions across unrelated files.
Do not publish documentation automation without reviewing deployment commands.
Do not create separate pages for duplicate or near-identical topics.

faq.question: Can this skill write documentation content?
faq.answer: It mainly organizes structure and tooling. Pair it with a writing skill for full content creation.

faq.question: Which documentation tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It includes patterns for MkDocs, Docusaurus, Sphinx, TypeDoc, GitHub Actions, and pre-commit linting.

faq.question: Is it safe to let it run commands?
faq.answer: Review commands first. Some examples install packages, start local servers, or deploy documentation.

faq.question: Can it support monorepos?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes monorepo layouts with shared docs and package-specific documentation.

faq.question: Does it check link accuracy?
faq.answer: No dedicated link checker is included. Use a documentation build or link validation tool.

faq.question: Can it migrate old documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan moves, consolidation, canonical pages, and redirect needs.
